# criblio_pack_lookups (Resource)

PackLookups Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_pack" "lookups_pack" {
  id           = "pack-with-lookups"
  group_id     = "default"
  description  = "Pack with lookups"
  disabled     = true
  display_name = "Pack from source"
  source       = "file:/opt/cribl_data/failover/groups/default/default/HelloPacks"
  version      = "1.0.0"
}


resource "criblio_pack_lookups" "my_packlookups" {
  content     = "column1, column2, column3, column4"
  description = "my_description"
  group_id    = "default"
  id          = "my_id"
  mode        = "memory"
  tags        = "my_tags"
  pack        = criblio_pack.lookups_pack.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `pack` (String)

### Optional

- `content` (String) File content.
- `description` (String)
- `mode` (String)
- `tags` (String)

### Read-Only

- `pending_task` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--pending_task))
- `version` (String) Unique string generated for each modification of this lookup

<a id="nestedatt--pending_task"></a>
### Nested Schema for `pending_task`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Task ID (generated).
- `type` (String) Task type
- `error` (String) Error message if task has failed

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_pack_lookups.my_criblio_pack_lookups
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "myExistingGroupId"
    id       = "myUniqueLookupIdToCRUD"
    pack     = "myExistingPackId"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_pack_lookups.my_criblio_pack_lookups '{"group_id": "myExistingGroupId", "id": "myUniqueLookupIdToCRUD", "pack": "myExistingPackId"}'
```
